title: Dissanthelium atropidiforme
taxonomy: Dissanthelium atropidiforme is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Dissanthelium, which is a group of grasses that are typically found in North America. Dissanthelium atropidiforme is further classified as part of the subfamily Pooideae. Within the genus Dissanthelium, it is often grouped with other species that share similar characteristics. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Dissanthelium atropidiforme from other species within the plant kingdom.

intro: Dissanthelium atropidiforme, commonly known as Atropidion grass or Purple Woodreed, is a perennial grass native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dissanthelium atropidiforme include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Dissanthelium atropidiforme is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, to support respiratory health and provide relief from congestion and coughs.
